title: Yelp API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Yelp API key detected. This key provides authenticated access to Yelp Fusion API including business search, business details, reviews, photos, and user authentication. Unauthorized access can lead to API quota abuse, business data scraping, review manipulation, and potential violation of Yelp's terms of service. Immediately revoke this key from your Yelp Developer account and rotate credentials using secure environment variables.

Yelp access token detected. This token grants access to Yelp API services with potentially extended permissions including business data access, review posting, and user account operations. Exposing Yelp tokens enables unauthorized API usage, business data extraction, and potential abuse of Yelp's platform services. Revoke immediately from your Yelp Developer account and implement secure credential storage.

Yelp client ID or application identifier detected. These identifiers are used for OAuth authentication and application registration with Yelp Fusion API. While less sensitive than API keys, exposing client IDs can reveal application configuration details and enable targeted attacks against your Yelp integrations. Store securely using environment variables or configuration management.
